{
  "status": "ready",
  "problem_statement": "Design a reproducible CIFAR-10 image-classification study comparing a ResNet-18 baseline against a ResNet-18 augmented with one lightweight attention module. The study must use public data and public implementations, and must report accuracy, latency, ablations, and stopping criteria.",
  "scope": "In scope: identification of a reproducible ResNet-18 CIFAR-10 baseline and a single lightweight attention module with a public implementation; a method to compare accuracy and latency, and to run ablations and define stopping criteria. Out of scope: non-CIFAR-10 datasets, non-ResNet-18 backbones, multi-module attention stacks, private or unreleased code/data, and deployment-specific hardware optimization.",
  "research_questions": [
    "What public ResNet-18 CIFAR-10 training setup and reported accuracy/latency can serve as a reproducible baseline?",
    "Which single lightweight attention modules have public implementations and have been evaluated on CIFAR-10 with ResNet-style backbones?",
    "What accuracy and latency tradeoffs have been reported for lightweight attention modules on CIFAR-10, and what ablations are standard?"
  ],

{
  "status": "ready",
  "problem_statement": "Design a reproducible CIFAR-10 image-classification study comparing a ResNet-18 baseline with one lightweight attention module, using public data and public implementations, and reporting accuracy, latency, ablations, and stopping criteria.",
  "scope": "CIFAR-10 supervised image classification. Baseline: ResNet-18 adapted for 32x32 CIFAR images. Intervention: a single lightweight attention module (e.g., SE, CBAM, ECA, or similar channel/spatial attention) integrated into the ResNet-18 backbone. Evaluation: top-1 accuracy, inference latency, ablation of attention placement/configuration, and explicit stopping criteria. Public data and public implementations only.",
  "research_questions": [
    "What public ResNet-18 CIFAR-10 implementations and training recipes are reproducible and report accuracy, latency, and stopping criteria?",
    "Which lightweight attention modules have public implementations compatible with ResNet-18 and have been evaluated on CIFAR-10 with reported accuracy and parameter/FLOP/latency overhead?",
    "What ablation design and stopping criteria are standard for CIFAR-10 ResNet attention studies to ensure fair,

{
  "schema_version": "0.1",
  "status": "ready",
  "clarification_question": "Which lightweight attention module should be used (e.g., SE, CBAM, ECA), or should the plan select one based on retrieved evidence?",
  "problem_statement": "Design a reproducible CIFAR-10 image-classification study comparing a ResNet-18 baseline with one lightweight attention module. The study must use public data and public implementations, and report accuracy, latency, ablations, and stopping criteria.",
  "scope": "CIFAR-10 image classification using a ResNet-18 backbone adapted for 32x32 inputs. The study compares the baseline against a single lightweight attention module inserted into the backbone. Evaluation includes top-1 accuracy, inference latency, ablation of module placement or hyperparameters, and explicit training stopping criteria. All data, code, and pretrained checkpoints (if any) must come from public sources.",
  "research_questions": [
    "What is the reproducible top-1 accuracy of a ResNet-18 baseline on CIFAR-10 under standard training settings, and what public implementation can be used as the reference baseline?",
